body: tracker.ready() calls dependency_closed() for each dep, which calls load_issue() and parses the JSON. For ready_issues() this means O(open_issues * avg_deps) file reads. Similarly, summary() has its own optimization (set of closed_ids) but ready() and ready_issues() don't share it. Consider building a state lookup cache (issue_id -> state) at the start of a command to avoid repeated loads.
title: ready() loads each dependency issue from disk individually
